saints.en: Forefeast of the Nativity of Christ. St. Martyr Juliana

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизми нема. На “Господи, взиваю я” 6 стихир: 3 передсв., 3 мчц.; Сл., І нині: передсв. Стиховня передсв., Сл., І нині: передсв. По “Отче наш”: тропар мчц., Сл., І нині: передсв. Відпуст середній з помин. дня і мчц.
Утреня: по “Бог Господь”: тропар передсв. (2), Сл.: мчц., І нині: передсв. Катизми черг., з сідал. передсв. Пс. 50. Канон передсв. та мчц. Катавасія тільки по 3-ій, 6-ій, 8-ій та 9-ій піснях – ірмос канону мчц. По 3-ій – конд.-ікос і сідал. мчц., Сл., І нині: сідал. передсв. По 6-ій – конд.-ікос передсв. По 9-ій – Світил. мчц., Сл., І нині: світил. передсв. На Хвалитних – 4 стихири передсв., Сл.: передсв., І нині: передсв. Стиховня передсв., Сл., І нині: передсв. По “Отче наш”: тропар мчц., Сл., І нині: передсв.
Часи: на всіх - тропар передсв.; на 3 – Сл.: тр. мчц. та 9 – Сл.: тр. мчц. Кондаки: 1 та 6 – передсв.; 3 і 9 – мчц.
Літургія: антифони повсякденні. Тропар передсв., Сл., І нині: конд. передсв.; все інше – дня. Ап. – Євр. 329 зач.; 11, 17-23.27-31. Єв. – Мр. 42 зач.; 9, 42-10, 1.

troparion_en.text: Prepare, O Bethlehem, for Eden has been opened to all! /  Adorn yourself, O Ephratha, for the Tree of Life blossoms forth from the Virgin in the cave! /  Her womb is a spiritual paradise planted with the Divine Fruit; / if we eat of it, we shall live forever and not die like Adam. /  Christ is born to raise up again what fell in former times, His image.

kontakion_en.text: He who holds the earth in the hollow of His hand / now is beheld in Bethlehem wrapped in swaddling bands. / We offer pre-festal hymns to His Mother, who maternally rejoices / having held to her bosom the Son of God.

matched_oca_saint: Forefeast of the Nativity of our Lord

oca_saints.name: Forefeast of the Nativity of our Lord

troparion.text: Prepare, O Bethlehem, for Eden has been opened to all! /  Adorn yourself, O Ephratha, for the Tree of Life blossoms forth from the Virgin in the cave! /  Her womb is a spiritual paradise planted with the Divine Fruit; / if we eat of it, we shall live forever and not die like Adam. /  Christ is born to raise up again what fell in former times, His image.

kontakion.text: He who holds the earth in the hollow of His hand / now is beheld in Bethlehem wrapped in swaddling bands. / We offer pre-festal hymns to His Mother, who maternally rejoices / having held to her bosom the Son of God.

oca_saints.name: Virgin Martyr Juliana of Nicomedia, and 500 men and 130 women with her

troparion.text: All-blameless bride and venerable trophy-bearer, / you are wedded to the Word of the immortal Father, O glorious Juliana. / For having wisely disdained your mortal bridegroom, / you strove beyond nature to destroy the serpent, / and now you delight in the joys of your Bridegroom!

kontakion.text: You were a beautiful virgin, wise Juliana, / and as your soul was wounded with divine love, / your body was also pierced with the wounds of martyrdom adorning you as a bride of Christ and His martyr. / Now as you dwell in the heavenly bridal chamber, / you pray for us all.

oca_saints.name: Repose of Saint Peter, Metropolitan of Moscow, Wonderworker of All Russia

troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Peter, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

Living devoutly according to the Law, and adorned with good deeds,  /  you appeared as a hard diamond, O Holy Right-believing Princess Juliana,  /  despising perishable glory and bodily beauty, /  you defeated the insidious Enemy, and you were martyred for the sake of chastity. /  Therefore, you were crowned by Christ God with an imperishable and eternal crown,  /  and now you rejoice in the assembly of the Martyrs.  /  Therefore, we cry out to you:  /  “Entreat Christ God for all of us, who with faith and love revere your sufferings.”

kontakion.text: Today let us sing to God, the Benefactor of all,  / Who glorified the divinely-wise Juliana in Russia,  /  that by her prayers He may grant to our souls  / the forgiveness of sins and great mercy.

oca_saints.name: Martyr Themistocles of Myra in Lycia

troparion.text: Your holy martyr Themistocles, O Lord, / through his suffering has received an incorruptible crown from You, our God. / For having Your strength, he laid low his adversaries, / and shattered the powerless boldness of demons. / Through his intercessions, save our souls!

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
You appeared as a bright star announcing Christ with your radiance,  / which is repulsive to this world, O Martyr Themistocles;  / extinguishing the allure of false gods,  / you enlighten the faithful, /  always interceding for us all.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Macarius the Faster, Abbot of the Khakhuli Monastery

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Macarius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!
